leadText: Urshifu (Japanese: ウーラオス Wulaosu) is a Fighting-type Legendary Pokémon introduced during Generation VIII in The Isle of Armor. It evolves from Kubfu when exposed to the scroll in one of the Towers of Two Fists. The form Kubfu evolves into depends on the scroll it chooses. In its Single Strike Style, it is dual-type Fighting/Dark. Kubfu evolves into this form when exposed to the Scroll of Darkness in the Tower of Darkness. In its Rapid Strike Style, it is dual-type Fighting/Water. Kubfu evolves into this form when exposed to the Scroll of Waters in the Tower of Waters. (Refer to Game data→Evolution data for more details.) Urshifu has two Gigantamax forms, one for each style. It can Gigantamax into Gigantamax Urshifu if it has the Gigantamax Factor. Urshifu is the game mascot of The Isle of Armor, appearing on the boxarts of the Pokémon Sword and Shield Expansion Pass.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Contents 1 Biology 1.1 Forms 1.1.1 Single Strike Style 1.1.2 Rapid Strike Style 1.1.3 Gigantamax Urshifu (Single Strike Style) 1.1.4 Gigantamax Urshifu (Rapid Strike Style) 1.2 Evolution Biology [ edit | edit source ] Urshifu is a large, bipedal Pokémon resembling a humanoid black bear with a wushu motif. It has a muscular build with long legs and long arms with large paws and claws for striking. Its body and head are covered in gray, black, and white fur, minus the yellow muzzle. The front of Urshifu's pelt slightly resembles a traditional frog buttoned/braid buttoned hanging off ends on both front and back. The fur on Urshifu's head is spikier and flares into a flowing white and black headband. The backs of Urshifu's hands, its knuckles, and the tops of its feet are also white-colored. Urshifu is the evolved form of the secret armor of the Isle of Armor 's Master Dojo , Kubfu . Its fighting style and personality are determined by how it trained and which one of the special scrolls it reads from one of the Towers of Two Fists , after which it reaches its full potential through either the path of darkness or the path of waters. It is also known to enjoy eating honey , particularly Max Honey found in Honeycalm Island . Wild Urshifu live in mountainous areas far away from the Galar region , training its legs and moves by racing across sheer cliffs. Urshifu is the only known Pokémon that can have Unseen Fist as its Ability . Forms [ edit | edit source ] Urshifu has two forms, Single Strike Style and Rapid Strike Style, each with unique Gigantamax forms. The two styles appear nearly identical to each other, with the main visual differences being the look of the fur on top of its head, and the posing of both of its fists and legs. Single Strike Style [ edit | edit source ] Appearance-wise, the fur on top of Single Strike Style Urshifu's head and on its legs flows up, while its headband flares upward and backward. It has its front fist raised, back fist lowered, and both feet planted. Single Strike Style Urshifu prefers battling without holding back, and often employs one-hit KO strategies. If enraged, it will mercilessly attack the opponent until it is utterly crushed. Its movements are direct and involve rushing in a straight line. When it is a certain distance away from an opponent, it quickly leaps in to unleash a powerful blow . [ 1 ] Single Strike Style Urshifu is the only known Pokémon capable of learning the move Wicked Blow . Rapid Strike Style [ edit | edit source ] Appearance-wise, the fur on top of Rapid Strike Style Urshifu's head and on its legs flows down, while its headband flares forward and outward. Rapid Strike Style Urshifu has its back fist raised, front fist lowered, and front foot lifted off the ground. Rapid Strike Style Urshifu prefers calmly observing its opponents and their strengths while dodging. Its movements and attacks have been compared to a river, flowing but unyielding. It uses flowing movements to ward off an opponent's attack before overwhelming them with rapid strikes . [ 1 ] Rapid Strike Style Urshifu is the only known Pokémon capable of learning the move Surging Strikes . Gigantamax Urshifu (Single Strike Style) [ edit | edit source ] When Single Strike Style Urshifu Gigantamaxes , the patterns on its fur become significantly accentuated, its headband lengthens, and its eyes and mouth take on a white glow. In general, parts of its fur that used to be gray are now white, and parts that used to be black are now red. In addition, Urshifu's leg fur and headband become spikier. Gigantamax Single Strike Style Urshifu has been called "the very embodiment of rage" and "a divine emissary that banishes evil spirits with its fury." [ 2 ] It is believed that Gigantamax Single Strike Style Urshifu can rid the world of its malevolence through its terrifying expression and shouting. The shockwaves released from its fists can blow away Dynamax Pokémon with just one hit. Gigantamax Single Strike Style Urshifu is the only known Pokémon capable of using the G-Max Move G-Max One Blow . Gigantamax Urshifu (Rapid Strike Style) [ edit | edit source ] When Rapid Strike Style Urshifu Gigantamaxes , the patterns on its fur become significantly accentuated, its headband lengthens, and its eyes and mouth take on a white glow. In general, parts of its fur that used to be gray are now white, and parts that used to be black are now blue. In addition, Urshifu's leg fur and headband become rounder and less spiky. Gigantamax Rapid Strike Style Urshifu maintains a perfect one-legged stance in a calm, unflinching manner. Opponents who face it may either feel greatly agitated or experience a sense of relief as if freed from evil spirits. [ 2 ] Gigantamax Rapid Strike Style Urshifu could supposedly destroy those with evil in their hearts by simply glaring at them. Gigantamax Rapid Strike Style Urshifu is the only known Pokémon capable of using the G-Max Move G-Max Rapid Flow . Urshifu (Single Strike Style) Gigantamax Urshifu (Single Strike Style) Urshifu (Rapid Strike Style) Gigantamax Urshifu (Rapid Strike Style) Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Urshifu evolves from Kubfu . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Kubfu Fighting → First Evolution Urshifu Single Strike Style Fighting Dark → First Evolution Urshifu Rapid Strike Style Fighting Water ↑ 1.0 1.1 Urshifu | Pokémon Sword and Pokémon Shield ↑ 2.0 2.1 Gigantamax Urshifu | Pokémon Sword and Pokémon Shield

As a Kubfu , it serves as the "secret armor" of the Master Dojo and is given to the player by Mustard after passing his three trials. It is later evolved into Single Strike Style or a Rapid Strike Style Urshifu after they defeat Mustard and his own Kubfu at the final floor of the Tower of Darkness or the Tower of Waters , by examining the Scroll of Darkness or the Scroll of Waters . The player later unlocks Urshifu's Gigantamax potential and has the chance to use it against Mustard's own Urshifu in the final battle against him. Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] This Pokémon was unavailable prior to Generation VIII. Generation VIII Galar #— Isle of Armor #101 Crown Tundra #— Sinnoh #— Hisui #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Brilliant Diamond, Shining Pearl , and Legends: Arceus . Single Strike Style Sword This form of Urshifu is a strong believer in the one-hit KO. Its strategy is to leap in close to foes and land a devastating blow with a hardened fist. Shield Inhabiting the mountains of a distant region, this Pokémon races across sheer cliffs, training its legs and refining its moves . Rapid Strike Style Sword It's believed that this Pokémon modeled its fighting style on the flow of a river—sometimes rapid, sometimes calm. Shield This form of Urshifu is a strong believer in defeating foes by raining many blows down on them. Its strikes are nonstop, flowing like a river. Gigantamax Single Strike Style Sword People call it the embodiment of rage. It's said that this Pokémon's terrifying expression and shout will rid the world of malevolence. Shield The energy released by this Pokémon's fists forms shock waves that can blow away Dynamax Pokémon in just one hit. Gigantamax Rapid Strike Style Sword As it waits for the right moment to unleash its Gigantamax power, this Pokémon maintains a perfect one-legged stance. It won't even twitch. Shield All it takes is a glare from this Pokémon to take the lives of those with evil in their hearts—or so they say. Generation IX Paldea #— Kitakami #— Blueberry #— Lumiose #— Hyperspace #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Generation IX . Bold indicates a move that gets STAB when used by Urshifu Italic indicates a move that gets STAB only when used by an Evolution of Urshifu Side game data [ edit | edit source ] Single Strike Style [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon GO Base HP: 225 Base Attack: 254 Base Defense: 177 Egg Distance: N/A Buddy Distance: 20 km Evolution Requirement: N/A Fast Attacks: Rock Smash , Counter , Sucker Punch Charged Attacks: Brick Break , Dynamic Punch , Close Combat , Payback Rapid Strike Style [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon GO Base HP: 225 Base Attack: 254 Base Defense: 177 Egg Distance: N/A Buddy Distance: 20 km Evolution Requirement: N/A Fast Attacks: Rock Smash , Counter , Waterfall Charged Attacks: Brick Break , Dynamic Punch , Close Combat , Aqua Jet Gigantamax Single Strike Style [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon GO Base HP: 225 Base Attack: 254 Base Defense: 177 Egg Distance: N/A Buddy Distance: 20 km Evolution Requirement: N/A Fast Attacks: N/A Charged Attacks: G-Max One Blow Gigantamax Rapid Strike Style [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon GO Base HP: 225 Base Attack: 254 Base Defense: 177 Egg Distance: N/A Buddy Distance: 20 km Evolution Requirement: N/A Fast Attacks: N/A Charged Attacks: G-Max Rapid Flow Form data [ edit | edit source ] Urshifu has two non-interchangeable forms: Single Strike Style and Rapid Strike Style. The two forms share the same in-game model, and are only animated differently, but have different types , with Single Strike Style being dual-type Fighting / Dark and Rapid Strike Style being dual-type Fighting / Water . They also learn different moves and have different cries and Gigantamax forms. Moves in italics do no damage. Gigantamax [ edit | edit source ] Urshifu has two Gigantamax forms, one for each Style. Gigantamax Factor Single Strike Style Urshifu Fighting Dark Dynamax Band ↔ Lasts for three turns Gigantamax Urshifu (Single Strike Style) Urshifu Fighting Dark Gigantamax Factor Rapid Strike Style Urshifu Fighting Water Dynamax Band ↔ Lasts for three turns Gigantamax Urshifu (Rapid Strike Style) Urshifu Fighting Water Evolution data [ edit | edit source ] In Generation VIII , the form of Urshifu that Kubfu evolves into is dependent on which of the Towers of Two Fists it evolved at, with it evolving into Single Strike Style at the Tower of Darkness or Rapid Strike Style at the Tower of Waters . In Generation IX , Kubfu evolves into Single Strike Style when exposed to the Scroll of Darkness or Rapid Strike Style when exposed to the Scroll of Waters . Unevolved Kubfu Fighting Interact with Scroll of Darkness → First Evolution Urshifu Single Strike Style Fighting Dark Interact with Scroll of Waters → First Evolution Urshifu Rapid Strike Style Fighting Water Sprites [ edit | edit source ] This Pokémon was unavailable prior to Generation VIII. Generation VIII Sword Shield Cross-Generation HOME For other sprites and images, please see Urshifu images on the Bulbagarden Archives .

sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry JN071 Urshifu Ash Ketchum 's Rotom Phone Urshifu, Single Strike Style . The Wushu Pokémon . A Fighting and Dark-type , and the evolved form of Kubfu . Its goal is to win with one strike. It charges at its opponent's chest and strikes with its highly trained fists . JN080 Urshifu Goh 's Rotom Phone Urshifu, Rapid Strike Style . The Wushu Pokémon. A Fighting and Water-type . They say it modeled its fighting style after a river—sometimes gently flowing and sometimes swirling and churning.
